changes.description: A summary of the new and changed values when an object is created, updated, or deleted

object1.description: For create, update, and delete events this is the object type that was affected. For associate and disassociate events this is the object type associated or disassociated with object2.

object2.description: Unpopulated for create, update, and delete events. For associate and disassociate events this is the object type that object1 is being associated with.

object_association.description: When present, shows the field name of the role or relationship that changed.

object_type.description: When present, shows the model on which the role or relationship was defined.

operation.description:
The action taken with respect to the given object(s).

* `create` - Entity Created
* `update` - Entity Updated
* `delete` - Entity Deleted
* `associate` - Entity Associated with another Entity
* `disassociate` - Entity was Disassociated with another Entity

enabled_value.description: Only used when enabled_var is set. Value when the host is considered enabled. For example if enabled_var="status.power_state"and enabled_value="powered_on" with host variables:{   "status": {     "power_state": "powered_on",     "created": "2018-02-01T08:00:00.000000Z:00",     "healthy": true    },    "name": "foobar",    "ip_address": "192.168.2.1"}The host would be marked enabled. If power_state where any value other than powered_on then the host would be disabled when imported. If the key is not found then the host will be enabled

enabled_var.description: Retrieve the enabled state from the given dict of host variables. The enabled variable may be specified as "foo.bar", in which case the lookup will traverse into nested dicts, equivalent to: from_dict.get("foo", {}).get("bar", default)

* `password` - Resource owner password-based

client_type.description:
Set to Public or Confidential depending on how secure the client device is.

* `confidential` - Confidential
* `public` - Public

organization.description: Organization containing this application.

skip_authorization.description: Set True to skip authorization step for completely trusted applications.

scope.description: Allowed scopes, further restricts user's permissions. Must be a simple space-separated string with allowed scopes ['read', 'write'].

default_environment.description: The default execution environment for jobs run by this organization.

max_hosts.description: Maximum number of hosts allowed to be managed by this organization.

opa_query_path.description: The query path for the OPA policy to evaluate prior to job execution. The query path should be formatted as package/rule.

allow_override.description: Allow changing the SCM branch or revision in a job template that uses this project.

default_environment.description: The default execution environment for jobs run using this project.

local_path.description: Local path (relative to PROJECTS_ROOT) containing playbooks and related files for this project.

organization.description: The organization used to determine access to this template.

scm_branch.description: Specific branch, tag or commit to checkout.

scm_clean.description: Discard any local changes before syncing the project.

scm_refspec.description: For git projects, an additional refspec to fetch.

scm_track_submodules.description: Track submodules latest commits on defined branch.

scm_type.description:
Specifies the source control system used to store the project.

* `` - Manual
* `git` - Git
* `svn` - Subversion
* `insights` - Red Hat Insights
* `archive` - Remote Archive

scm_update_cache_timeout.description: The number of seconds after the last project update ran that a new project update will be launched as a job dependency.

scm_update_on_launch.description: Update the project when a job is launched that uses the project.

scm_url.description: The location where the project is stored.

signature_validation_credential.description: An optional credential used for validating files in the project against unexpected changes.

timeout.description: The amount of time (in seconds) to run before the task is canceled.

ansible_id.description: A unique ID identifying this resource by the resource server.

is_partially_migrated.description: A flag indicating that the resource has been copied into the resource server, but the service_id hasn't been updated yet.

service_id.description: ID of the service responsible for managing this resource.

content_type.description: String to use for references to this type from other models in the API.

items.description: String to use for references to this type from other models in the API.

execution_environment.description: The container image to be used for execution.

inventory.description: Inventory applied as a prompt, assuming job template prompts for inventory

rrule.description: A value representing the schedules iCal recurrence rule.

PatchedSettingSingletonRequest.description: Present a group of settings (by category) as a single object.

ACTIVITY_STREAM_ENABLED.description: Enable capturing activity for the activity stream.
ACTIVITY_STREAM_ENABLED.title: Enable Activity Stream

ACTIVITY_STREAM_ENABLED_FOR_INVENTORY_SY.description: Enable capturing activity for the activity stream when running inventory sync.
ACTIVITY_STREAM_ENABLED_FOR_INVENTORY_SY.title: Enable Activity Stream for Inventory Sync

AD_HOC_COMMANDS.description: List of modules allowed to be used by ad-hoc jobs.
AD_HOC_COMMANDS.title: Ansible Modules Allowed for Ad Hoc Jobs

ALLOW_JINJA_IN_EXTRA_VARS.description:
Ansible allows variable substitution via the Jinja2 templating language for --extra-vars. This poses a potential security risk where users with the ability to specify extra vars at job launch time can use Jinja2 templates to run arbitrary Python.  It is recommended that this value be set to "template" or "never".

* `always` - Always
* `never` - Never
* `template` - Only On Job Template Definitions
ALLOW_JINJA_IN_EXTRA_VARS.title: When can extra variables contain Jinja templates?

ALLOW_METRICS_FOR_ANONYMOUS_USERS.description: If true, anonymous users are allowed to poll metrics.
ALLOW_METRICS_FOR_ANONYMOUS_USERS.title: Allow anonymous users to poll metrics

ALLOW_OAUTH2_FOR_EXTERNAL_USERS.description: For security reasons, users from external auth providers (LDAP, SAML, SSO, Radius, and others) are not allowed to create OAuth2 tokens. To change this behavior, enable this setting. Existing tokens will not be deleted when this setting is toggled off.
ALLOW_OAUTH2_FOR_EXTERNAL_USERS.title: Allow External Users to Create OAuth2 Tokens

ANSIBLE_FACT_CACHE_TIMEOUT.description: Maximum time, in seconds, that stored Ansible facts are considered valid since the last time they were modified. Only valid, non-stale, facts will be accessible by a playbook. Note, this does not influence the deletion of ansible_facts from the database. Use a value of 0 to indicate that no timeout should be imposed.
ANSIBLE_FACT_CACHE_TIMEOUT.title: Per-Host Ansible Fact Cache Timeout

API_400_ERROR_LOG_FORMAT.default: status {status_code} received by user {user_name} attempting to access {url_path} from {remote_addr}
API_400_ERROR_LOG_FORMAT.description:
The format of logged messages when an API 4XX error occurs, the following variables will be substituted: 
status_code - The HTTP status code of the error
user_name - The user name attempting to use the API
url_path - The URL path to the API endpoint called
remote_addr - The remote address seen for the user
error - The error set by the api endpoint
Variables need to be in the format {<variable name>}.
API_400_ERROR_LOG_FORMAT.title: Log Format For API 4XX Errors

AUTH_BASIC_ENABLED.description: Enable HTTP Basic Auth for the API Browser.
AUTH_BASIC_ENABLED.title: Enable HTTP Basic Auth

AUTH_LDAP_1_BIND_DN.description: DN (Distinguished Name) of user to bind for all search queries. This is the system user account we will use to login to query LDAP for other user information. Refer to the documentation for example syntax.
AUTH_LDAP_1_BIND_DN.title: LDAP Bind DN

AUTH_LDAP_1_BIND_PASSWORD.description: Password used to bind LDAP user account.
AUTH_LDAP_1_BIND_PASSWORD.title: LDAP Bind Password

AUTH_LDAP_1_CONNECTION_OPTIONS.description: Additional options to set for the LDAP connection.  LDAP referrals are disabled by default (to prevent certain LDAP queries from hanging with AD). Option names should be strings (e.g. "OPT_REFERRALS"). Refer to https://www.python-ldap.org/doc/html/ldap.html#options for possible options and values that can be set.
AUTH_LDAP_1_CONNECTION_OPTIONS.title: LDAP Connection Options

AUTH_LDAP_1_DENY_GROUP.description: Group DN denied from login. If specified, user will not be allowed to login if a member of this group.  Only one deny group is supported.
AUTH_LDAP_1_DENY_GROUP.title: LDAP Deny Group

AUTH_LDAP_1_GROUP_SEARCH.description: Users are mapped to organizations based on their membership in LDAP groups. This setting defines the LDAP search query to find groups. Unlike the user search, group search does not support LDAPSearchUnion.
AUTH_LDAP_1_GROUP_SEARCH.title: LDAP Group Search

AUTH_LDAP_1_GROUP_TYPE.description:
The group type may need to be changed based on the type of the LDAP server.  Values are listed at: https://django-auth-ldap.readthedocs.io/en/stable/groups.html#types-of-groups

* `PosixGroupType` - PosixGroupType
* `GroupOfNamesType` - GroupOfNamesType
* `GroupOfUniqueNamesType` - GroupOfUniqueNamesType
* `ActiveDirectoryGroupType` - ActiveDirectoryGroupType
* `OrganizationalRoleGroupType` - OrganizationalRoleGroupType
* `MemberDNGroupType` - MemberDNGroupType
* `NestedGroupOfNamesType` - NestedGroupOfNamesType
* `NestedGroupOfUniqueNamesType` - NestedGroupOfUniqueNamesType
* `NestedActiveDirectoryGroupType` - NestedActiveDirectoryGroupType
* `NestedOrganizationalRoleGroupType` - NestedOrganizationalRoleGroupType
* `NestedMemberDNGroupType` - NestedMemberDNGroupType
* `PosixUIDGroupType` - PosixUIDGroupType
AUTH_LDAP_1_GROUP_TYPE.title: LDAP Group Type

AUTH_LDAP_1_GROUP_TYPE_PARAMS.description: Key value parameters to send the chosen group type init method.
AUTH_LDAP_1_GROUP_TYPE_PARAMS.title: LDAP Group Type Parameters

AUTH_LDAP_1_ORGANIZATION_MAP.description: Mapping between organization admins/users and LDAP groups. This controls which users are placed into which organizations relative to their LDAP group memberships. Configuration details are available in the documentation.
AUTH_LDAP_1_ORGANIZATION_MAP.title: LDAP Organization Map

AUTH_LDAP_1_REQUIRE_GROUP.description: Group DN required to login. If specified, user must be a member of this group to login via LDAP. If not set, everyone in LDAP that matches the user search will be able to login to the service. Only one require group is supported.
AUTH_LDAP_1_REQUIRE_GROUP.title: LDAP Require Group

AUTH_LDAP_1_SERVER_URI.description: URI to connect to LDAP server, such as "ldap://ldap.example.com:389" (non-SSL) or "ldaps://ldap.example.com:636" (SSL). Multiple LDAP servers may be specified by separating with spaces or commas. LDAP authentication is disabled if this parameter is empty.
AUTH_LDAP_1_SERVER_URI.title: LDAP Server URI

AUTH_LDAP_1_START_TLS.description: Whether to enable TLS when the LDAP connection is not using SSL.
AUTH_LDAP_1_START_TLS.title: LDAP Start TLS

AUTH_LDAP_1_TEAM_MAP.description: Mapping between team members (users) and LDAP groups. Configuration details are available in the documentation.
AUTH_LDAP_1_TEAM_MAP.title: LDAP Team Map

AUTH_LDAP_1_USER_ATTR_MAP.description: Mapping of LDAP user schema to API user attributes. The default setting is valid for ActiveDirectory but users with other LDAP configurations may need to change the values. Refer to the documentation for additional details.
AUTH_LDAP_1_USER_ATTR_MAP.title: LDAP User Attribute Map

AUTH_LDAP_1_USER_DN_TEMPLATE.description: Alternative to user search, if user DNs are all of the same format. This approach is more efficient for user lookups than searching if it is usable in your organizational environment. If this setting has a value it will be used instead of AUTH_LDAP_USER_SEARCH.
AUTH_LDAP_1_USER_DN_TEMPLATE.title: LDAP User DN Template

AUTH_LDAP_1_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP.description: Retrieve users from a given group. At this time, superuser and system auditors are the only groups supported. Refer to the documentation for more detail.
AUTH_LDAP_1_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP.title: LDAP User Flags By Group

AUTH_LDAP_1_USER_SEARCH.description: LDAP search query to find users.  Any user that matches the given pattern will be able to login to the service.  The user should also be mapped into an organization (as defined in the AUTH_LDAP_ORGANIZATION_MAP setting).  If multiple search queries need to be supported use of "LDAPUnion" is possible. See the documentation for details.
AUTH_LDAP_1_USER_SEARCH.title: LDAP User Search

* `NestedActiveDirectoryGroupType` - NestedActiveDirectoryGroupType
* `NestedOrganizationalRoleGroupType` - NestedOrganizationalRoleGroupType
* `NestedMemberDNGroupType` - NestedMemberDNGroupType
* `PosixUIDGroupType` - PosixUIDGroupType
AUTH_LDAP_5_GROUP_TYPE.title: LDAP Group Type

AUTH_LDAP_5_GROUP_TYPE_PARAMS.description: Key value parameters to send the chosen group type init method.
AUTH_LDAP_5_GROUP_TYPE_PARAMS.title: LDAP Group Type Parameters

AUTH_LDAP_5_ORGANIZATION_MAP.description: Mapping between organization admins/users and LDAP groups. This controls which users are placed into which organizations relative to their LDAP group memberships. Configuration details are available in the documentation.
AUTH_LDAP_5_ORGANIZATION_MAP.title: LDAP Organization Map

AUTH_LDAP_5_REQUIRE_GROUP.description: Group DN required to login. If specified, user must be a member of this group to login via LDAP. If not set, everyone in LDAP that matches the user search will be able to login to the service. Only one require group is supported.
AUTH_LDAP_5_REQUIRE_GROUP.title: LDAP Require Group

AUTH_LDAP_5_SERVER_URI.description: URI to connect to LDAP server, such as "ldap://ldap.example.com:389" (non-SSL) or "ldaps://ldap.example.com:636" (SSL). Multiple LDAP servers may be specified by separating with spaces or commas. LDAP authentication is disabled if this parameter is empty.
AUTH_LDAP_5_SERVER_URI.title: LDAP Server URI

AUTH_LDAP_5_START_TLS.description: Whether to enable TLS when the LDAP connection is not using SSL.
AUTH_LDAP_5_START_TLS.title: LDAP Start TLS

AUTH_LDAP_5_TEAM_MAP.description: Mapping between team members (users) and LDAP groups. Configuration details are available in the documentation.
AUTH_LDAP_5_TEAM_MAP.title: LDAP Team Map

AUTH_LDAP_5_USER_ATTR_MAP.description: Mapping of LDAP user schema to API user attributes. The default setting is valid for ActiveDirectory but users with other LDAP configurations may need to change the values. Refer to the documentation for additional details.
AUTH_LDAP_5_USER_ATTR_MAP.title: LDAP User Attribute Map

AUTH_LDAP_5_USER_DN_TEMPLATE.description: Alternative to user search, if user DNs are all of the same format. This approach is more efficient for user lookups than searching if it is usable in your organizational environment. If this setting has a value it will be used instead of AUTH_LDAP_USER_SEARCH.
AUTH_LDAP_5_USER_DN_TEMPLATE.title: LDAP User DN Template

AUTH_LDAP_5_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP.description: Retrieve users from a given group. At this time, superuser and system auditors are the only groups supported. Refer to the documentation for more detail.
AUTH_LDAP_5_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP.title: LDAP User Flags By Group

AUTH_LDAP_5_USER_SEARCH.description: LDAP search query to find users.  Any user that matches the given pattern will be able to login to the service.  The user should also be mapped into an organization (as defined in the AUTH_LDAP_ORGANIZATION_MAP setting).  If multiple search queries need to be supported use of "LDAPUnion" is possible. See the documentation for details.
AUTH_LDAP_5_USER_SEARCH.title: LDAP User Search

AUTH_LDAP_BIND_DN.description: DN (Distinguished Name) of user to bind for all search queries. This is the system user account we will use to login to query LDAP for other user information. Refer to the documentation for example syntax.
AUTH_LDAP_BIND_DN.title: LDAP Bind DN

AUTH_LDAP_BIND_PASSWORD.description: Password used to bind LDAP user account.
AUTH_LDAP_BIND_PASSWORD.title: LDAP Bind Password

AUTH_LDAP_CONNECTION_OPTIONS.description: Additional options to set for the LDAP connection.  LDAP referrals are disabled by default (to prevent certain LDAP queries from hanging with AD). Option names should be strings (e.g. "OPT_REFERRALS"). Refer to https://www.python-ldap.org/doc/html/ldap.html#options for possible options and values that can be set.
AUTH_LDAP_CONNECTION_OPTIONS.title: LDAP Connection Options

AUTH_LDAP_DENY_GROUP.description: Group DN denied from login. If specified, user will not be allowed to login if a member of this group.  Only one deny group is supported.
AUTH_LDAP_DENY_GROUP.title: LDAP Deny Group

AUTH_LDAP_GROUP_SEARCH.description: Users are mapped to organizations based on their membership in LDAP groups. This setting defines the LDAP search query to find groups. Unlike the user search, group search does not support LDAPSearchUnion.
AUTH_LDAP_GROUP_SEARCH.title: LDAP Group Search

AUTH_LDAP_GROUP_TYPE.description:
The group type may need to be changed based on the type of the LDAP server.  Values are listed at: https://django-auth-ldap.readthedocs.io/en/stable/groups.html#types-of-groups

* `PosixGroupType` - PosixGroupType
* `GroupOfNamesType` - GroupOfNamesType
* `GroupOfUniqueNamesType` - GroupOfUniqueNamesType
* `ActiveDirectoryGroupType` - ActiveDirectoryGroupType
* `OrganizationalRoleGroupType` - OrganizationalRoleGroupType
* `MemberDNGroupType` - MemberDNGroupType
* `NestedGroupOfNamesType` - NestedGroupOfNamesType
* `NestedGroupOfUniqueNamesType` - NestedGroupOfUniqueNamesType
* `NestedActiveDirectoryGroupType` - NestedActiveDirectoryGroupType
* `NestedOrganizationalRoleGroupType` - NestedOrganizationalRoleGroupType
* `NestedMemberDNGroupType` - NestedMemberDNGroupType
* `PosixUIDGroupType` - PosixUIDGroupType
AUTH_LDAP_GROUP_TYPE.title: LDAP Group Type

AUTH_LDAP_GROUP_TYPE_PARAMS.description: Key value parameters to send the chosen group type init method.
AUTH_LDAP_GROUP_TYPE_PARAMS.title: LDAP Group Type Parameters

AUTH_LDAP_ORGANIZATION_MAP.description: Mapping between organization admins/users and LDAP groups. This controls which users are placed into which organizations relative to their LDAP group memberships. Configuration details are available in the documentation.
AUTH_LDAP_ORGANIZATION_MAP.title: LDAP Organization Map

AUTH_LDAP_REQUIRE_GROUP.description: Group DN required to login. If specified, user must be a member of this group to login via LDAP. If not set, everyone in LDAP that matches the user search will be able to login to the service. Only one require group is supported.
AUTH_LDAP_REQUIRE_GROUP.title: LDAP Require Group

AUTH_LDAP_SERVER_URI.description: URI to connect to LDAP server, such as "ldap://ldap.example.com:389" (non-SSL) or "ldaps://ldap.example.com:636" (SSL). Multiple LDAP servers may be specified by separating with spaces or commas. LDAP authentication is disabled if this parameter is empty.
AUTH_LDAP_SERVER_URI.title: LDAP Server URI

AUTH_LDAP_START_TLS.description: Whether to enable TLS when the LDAP connection is not using SSL.
AUTH_LDAP_START_TLS.title: LDAP Start TLS

AUTH_LDAP_TEAM_MAP.description: Mapping between team members (users) and LDAP groups. Configuration details are available in the documentation.
AUTH_LDAP_TEAM_MAP.title: LDAP Team Map

AUTH_LDAP_USER_ATTR_MAP.description: Mapping of LDAP user schema to API user attributes. The default setting is valid for ActiveDirectory but users with other LDAP configurations may need to change the values. Refer to the documentation for additional details.
AUTH_LDAP_USER_ATTR_MAP.title: LDAP User Attribute Map

AUTH_LDAP_USER_DN_TEMPLATE.description: Alternative to user search, if user DNs are all of the same format. This approach is more efficient for user lookups than searching if it is usable in your organizational environment. If this setting has a value it will be used instead of AUTH_LDAP_USER_SEARCH.
AUTH_LDAP_USER_DN_TEMPLATE.title: LDAP User DN Template

AUTH_LDAP_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP.description: Retrieve users from a given group. At this time, superuser and system auditors are the only groups supported. Refer to the documentation for more detail.
AUTH_LDAP_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP.title: LDAP User Flags By Group

AUTH_LDAP_USER_SEARCH.description: LDAP search query to find users.  Any user that matches the given pattern will be able to login to the service.  The user should also be mapped into an organization (as defined in the AUTH_LDAP_ORGANIZATION_MAP setting).  If multiple search queries need to be supported use of "LDAPUnion" is possible. See the documentation for details.
AUTH_LDAP_USER_SEARCH.title: LDAP User Search

AUTOMATION_ANALYTICS_LAST_ENTRIES.title: Last gathered entries from the data collection service of Automation Analytics

AUTOMATION_ANALYTICS_LAST_GATHER.title: Last gather date for Automation Analytics.

AUTOMATION_ANALYTICS_URL.description: This setting is used to to configure the upload URL for data collection for Automation Analytics.
AUTOMATION_ANALYTICS_URL.title: Automation Analytics upload URL

AWX_ANSIBLE_CALLBACK_PLUGINS.description: List of paths to search for extra callback plugins to be used when running jobs. Enter one path per line.
AWX_ANSIBLE_CALLBACK_PLUGINS.title: Ansible Callback Plugins

AWX_CLEANUP_PATHS.description: Enable or Disable TMP Dir cleanup
AWX_CLEANUP_PATHS.title: Enable or Disable tmp dir cleanup

AWX_COLLECTIONS_ENABLED.description: Allows collections to be dynamically downloaded from a requirements.yml file for SCM projects.
AWX_COLLECTIONS_ENABLED.title: Enable Collection(s) Download

AWX_ISOLATION_BASE_PATH.description: The directory in which the service will create new temporary directories for job execution and isolation (such as credential files).
AWX_ISOLATION_BASE_PATH.title: Job execution path

AWX_ISOLATION_SHOW_PATHS.description: List of paths that would otherwise be hidden to expose to isolated jobs. Enter one path per line. Volumes will be mounted from the execution node to the container. The supported format is HOST-DIR[:CONTAINER-DIR[:OPTIONS]].
AWX_ISOLATION_SHOW_PATHS.title: Paths to expose to isolated jobs

AWX_MOUNT_ISOLATED_PATHS_ON_K8S.description: Expose paths via hostPath for the Pods created by a Container Group. HostPath volumes present many security risks, and it is a best practice to avoid the use of HostPaths when possible.
AWX_MOUNT_ISOLATED_PATHS_ON_K8S.title: Expose host paths for Container Groups

AWX_ROLES_ENABLED.description: Allows roles to be dynamically downloaded from a requirements.yml file for SCM projects.
AWX_ROLES_ENABLED.title: Enable Role Download

AWX_RUNNER_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS.description: Only applies to jobs running in a Container Group. If not 0, send a message every so-many seconds to keep connection open.
AWX_RUNNER_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS.title: K8S Ansible Runner Keep-Alive Message Interval

AWX_SHOW_PLAYBOOK_LINKS.description: Follow symbolic links when scanning for playbooks. Be aware that setting this to True can lead to infinite recursion if a link points to a parent directory of itself.
AWX_SHOW_PLAYBOOK_LINKS.title: Follow symlinks

AWX_TASK_ENV.description: Additional environment variables set for playbook runs, inventory updates, project updates, and notification sending.
AWX_TASK_ENV.title: Extra Environment Variables

BULK_HOST_MAX_CREATE.description: Max number of hosts to allow to be created in a single bulk action
BULK_HOST_MAX_CREATE.title: Max number of hosts to allow to be created in a single bulk action

BULK_HOST_MAX_DELETE.description: Max number of hosts to allow to be deleted in a single bulk action
BULK_HOST_MAX_DELETE.title: Max number of hosts to allow to be deleted in a single bulk action

BULK_JOB_MAX_LAUNCH.description: Max jobs to allow bulk jobs to launch
BULK_JOB_MAX_LAUNCH.title: Max jobs to allow bulk jobs to launch

CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS.description: If the service is behind a reverse proxy/load balancer, use this setting to configure the schema://addresses from which the service should trust Origin header values.
CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS.title: CSRF Trusted Origins List

CUSTOM_LOGIN_INFO.description: If needed, you can add specific information (such as a legal notice or a disclaimer) to a text box in the login modal using this setting. Any content added must be in plain text or an HTML fragment, as other markup languages are not supported.

CUSTOM_LOGO.description: To set up a custom logo, provide a file that you create. For the custom logo to look its best, use a .png file with a transparent background. GIF, PNG and JPEG formats are supported.

CUSTOM_VENV_PATHS.description: Paths where Tower will look for custom virtual environments (in addition to /var/lib/awx/venv/). Enter one path per line.
CUSTOM_VENV_PATHS.title: Custom virtual environment paths

DEFAULT_CONTAINER_RUN_OPTIONS.description: List of options to pass to podman run example: ['--network', 'slirp4netns:enable_ipv6=true', '--log-level', 'debug']
DEFAULT_CONTAINER_RUN_OPTIONS.title: Container Run Options

DEFAULT_EXECUTION_ENVIRONMENT.description: The Execution Environment to be used when one has not been configured for a job template.
DEFAULT_EXECUTION_ENVIRONMENT.title: Global default execution environment

DEFAULT_INVENTORY_UPDATE_TIMEOUT.description: Maximum time in seconds to allow inventory updates to run. Use value of 0 to indicate that no timeout should be imposed. A timeout set on an individual inventory source will override this.

DEFAULT_JOB_IDLE_TIMEOUT.description: If no output is detected from ansible in this number of seconds the execution will be terminated. Use value of 0 to indicate that no idle timeout should be imposed.

DEFAULT_JOB_TIMEOUT.description: Maximum time in seconds to allow jobs to run. Use value of 0 to indicate that no timeout should be imposed. A timeout set on an individual job template will override this.

DEFAULT_PROJECT_UPDATE_TIMEOUT.description: Maximum time in seconds to allow project updates to run. Use value of 0 to indicate that no timeout should be imposed. A timeout set on an individual project will override this.

DISABLE_LOCAL_AUTH.description: Controls whether users are prevented from using the built-in authentication system. You probably want to do this if you are using an LDAP or SAML integration.
DISABLE_LOCAL_AUTH.title: Disable the built-in authentication system

EVENT_STDOUT_MAX_BYTES_DISPLAY.description: Maximum Size of Standard Output in bytes to display for a single job or ad hoc command event. `stdout` will end with `…` when truncated.
EVENT_STDOUT_MAX_BYTES_DISPLAY.title: Job Event Standard Output Maximum Display Size

GALAXY_IGNORE_CERTS.description: If set to true, certificate validation will not be done when installing content from any Galaxy server.
GALAXY_IGNORE_CERTS.title: Ignore Ansible Galaxy SSL Certificate Verification

GALAXY_TASK_ENV.description: Additional environment variables set for invocations of ansible-galaxy within project updates. Useful if you must use a proxy server for ansible-galaxy but not git.
GALAXY_TASK_ENV.title: Environment Variables for Galaxy Commands

INSIGHTS_TRACKING_STATE.description: Enables the service to gather data on automation and send it to Automation Analytics.
INSIGHTS_TRACKING_STATE.title: Gather data for Automation Analytics

LOCAL_PASSWORD_MIN_DIGITS.description: Minimum number of digit characters required in a local password. 0 means no minimum
LOCAL_PASSWORD_MIN_DIGITS.title: Minimum number of digit characters in local password

LOCAL_PASSWORD_MIN_LENGTH.description: Minimum number of characters required in a local password. 0 means no minimum
LOCAL_PASSWORD_MIN_LENGTH.title: Minimum number of characters in local password

LOCAL_PASSWORD_MIN_SPECIAL.description: Minimum number of special characters required in a local password. 0 means no minimum
LOCAL_PASSWORD_MIN_SPECIAL.title: Minimum number of special characters in local password

LOCAL_PASSWORD_MIN_UPPER.description: Minimum number of uppercase characters required in a local password. 0 means no minimum
LOCAL_PASSWORD_MIN_UPPER.title: Minimum number of uppercase characters in local password

LOGIN_REDIRECT_OVERRIDE.description: URL to which unauthorized users will be redirected to log in.  If blank, users will be sent to the login page.
LOGIN_REDIRECT_OVERRIDE.title: Login redirect override URL

LOG_AGGREGATOR_ACTION_MAX_DISK_USAGE_GB.description: Amount of data to store (in gigabytes) if an rsyslog action takes time to process an incoming message (defaults to 1). Equivalent to the rsyslogd queue.maxdiskspace setting on the action (e.g. omhttp). It stores files in the directory specified by LOG_AGGREGATOR_MAX_DISK_USAGE_PATH.
LOG_AGGREGATOR_ACTION_MAX_DISK_USAGE_GB.title: Maximum disk persistence for rsyslogd action queuing (in GB)

LOG_AGGREGATOR_ACTION_QUEUE_SIZE.description: Defines how large the rsyslog action queue can grow in number of messages stored. This can have an impact on memory utilization. When the queue reaches 75% of this number, the queue will start writing to disk (queue.highWatermark in rsyslog). When it reaches 90%, NOTICE, INFO, and DEBUG messages will start to be discarded (queue.discardMark with queue.discardSeverity=5).
LOG_AGGREGATOR_ACTION_QUEUE_SIZE.title: Maximum number of messages that can be stored in the log action queue

LOG_AGGREGATOR_ENABLED.description: Enable sending logs to external log aggregator.
LOG_AGGREGATOR_ENABLED.title: Enable External Logging

LOG_AGGREGATOR_HOST.description: Hostname/IP where external logs will be sent to.
LOG_AGGREGATOR_HOST.title: Logging Aggregator

LOG_AGGREGATOR_INDIVIDUAL_FACTS.description: If set, system tracking facts will be sent for each package, service, or other item found in a scan, allowing for greater search query granularity. If unset, facts will be sent as a single dictionary, allowing for greater efficiency in fact processing.
LOG_AGGREGATOR_INDIVIDUAL_FACTS.title: Log System Tracking Facts Individually

LOG_AGGREGATOR_LEVEL.description:
Level threshold used by log handler. Severities from lowest to highest are DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL. Messages less severe than the threshold will be ignored by log handler. (messages under category awx.anlytics ignore this setting)

* `DEBUG` - DEBUG
* `INFO` - INFO
* `WARNING` - WARNING
* `ERROR` - ERROR
* `CRITICAL` - CRITICAL
LOG_AGGREGATOR_LEVEL.title: Logging Aggregator Level Threshold

LOG_AGGREGATOR_LOGGERS.description:
List of loggers that will send HTTP logs to the collector, these can include any or all of: 
awx - service logs
activity_stream - activity stream records
job_events - callback data from Ansible job events
system_tracking - facts gathered from scan jobs
broadcast_websocket - errors pertaining to websockets broadcast metrics
job_lifecycle - logs related to processing of a job
LOG_AGGREGATOR_LOGGERS.title: Loggers Sending Data to Log Aggregator Form

LOG_AGGREGATOR_MAX_DISK_USAGE_PATH.description: Location to persist logs that should be retried after an outage of the external log aggregator (defaults to /var/lib/awx). Equivalent to the rsyslogd queue.spoolDirectory setting.
LOG_AGGREGATOR_MAX_DISK_USAGE_PATH.title: File system location for rsyslogd disk persistence

LOG_AGGREGATOR_PASSWORD.description: Password or authentication token for external log aggregator (if required; HTTP/s only).
LOG_AGGREGATOR_PASSWORD.title: Logging Aggregator Password/Token

LOG_AGGREGATOR_PORT.description: Port on Logging Aggregator to send logs to (if required and not provided in Logging Aggregator).
LOG_AGGREGATOR_PORT.title: Logging Aggregator Port

LOG_AGGREGATOR_PROTOCOL.description:
Protocol used to communicate with log aggregator.  HTTPS/HTTP assumes HTTPS unless http:// is explicitly used in the Logging Aggregator hostname.

* `https` - HTTPS/HTTP
* `tcp` - TCP
* `udp` - UDP
LOG_AGGREGATOR_PROTOCOL.title: Logging Aggregator Protocol

LOG_AGGREGATOR_RSYSLOGD_DEBUG.description: Enabled high verbosity debugging for rsyslogd.  Useful for debugging connection issues for external log aggregation.
LOG_AGGREGATOR_RSYSLOGD_DEBUG.title: Enable rsyslogd debugging

LOG_AGGREGATOR_TCP_TIMEOUT.description: Number of seconds for a TCP connection to external log aggregator to timeout. Applies to HTTPS and TCP log aggregator protocols.
LOG_AGGREGATOR_TCP_TIMEOUT.title: TCP Connection Timeout

LOG_AGGREGATOR_TYPE.description:
Format messages for the chosen log aggregator.

* `logstash` - logstash
* `splunk` - splunk
* `loggly` - loggly
* `sumologic` - sumologic
* `other` - other
LOG_AGGREGATOR_TYPE.title: Logging Aggregator Type

LOG_AGGREGATOR_USERNAME.description: Username for external log aggregator (if required; HTTP/s only).
LOG_AGGREGATOR_USERNAME.title: Logging Aggregator Username

LOG_AGGREGATOR_VERIFY_CERT.description: Flag to control enable/disable of certificate verification when LOG_AGGREGATOR_PROTOCOL is "https". If enabled, the log handler will verify certificate sent by external log aggregator before establishing connection.
LOG_AGGREGATOR_VERIFY_CERT.title: Enable/disable HTTPS certificate verification

MANAGE_ORGANIZATION_AUTH.description: Controls whether any Organization Admin has the privileges to create and manage users and teams. You may want to disable this ability if you are using an LDAP or SAML integration.
MANAGE_ORGANIZATION_AUTH.title: Organization Admins Can Manage Users and Teams

MAX_FORKS.description: Saving a Job Template with more than this number of forks will result in an error. When set to 0, no limit is applied.
MAX_FORKS.title: Maximum number of forks per job

MAX_UI_JOB_EVENTS.description: Maximum number of job events for the UI to retrieve within a single request.
MAX_UI_JOB_EVENTS.title: Max Job Events Retrieved by UI

MAX_WEBSOCKET_EVENT_RATE.description: Maximum number of messages to update the UI live job output with per second. Value of 0 means no limit.
MAX_WEBSOCKET_EVENT_RATE.title: Job Event Maximum Websocket Messages Per Second

OAUTH2_PROVIDER.description: DEPRECATED: This setting is for a legacy feature and will be removed. Use token-based authentication instead.Dictionary for customizing OAuth 2 timeouts, available items are `ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_SECONDS`, the duration of access tokens in the number of seconds, `AUTHORIZATION_CODE_EXPIRE_SECONDS`, the duration of authorization codes in the number of seconds, and `REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRE_SECONDS`, the duration of refresh tokens, after expired access tokens, in the number of seconds.
OAUTH2_PROVIDER.title: OAuth 2 Timeout Settings

OPA_AUTH_CA_CERT.description: The content of the CA certificate for mTLS authentication to the OPA server. Required when OPA_AUTH_TYPE is "Certificate".
OPA_AUTH_CA_CERT.title: OPA CA certificate content

OPA_AUTH_CLIENT_CERT.description: The content of the client certificate file for mTLS authentication to the OPA server. Required when OPA_AUTH_TYPE is "Certificate".
OPA_AUTH_CLIENT_CERT.title: OPA client certificate content

OPA_AUTH_CLIENT_KEY.description: The content of the client key for mTLS authentication to the OPA server. Required when OPA_AUTH_TYPE is "Certificate".
OPA_AUTH_CLIENT_KEY.title: OPA client key content

OPA_AUTH_CUSTOM_HEADERS.description: Optional custom headers included in requests to the OPA server. Defaults to empty dictionary ({}).
OPA_AUTH_CUSTOM_HEADERS.title: OPA custom authentication headers

OPA_AUTH_TOKEN.description: The token for authentication to the OPA server. Required when OPA_AUTH_TYPE is "Token". If an authorization header is defined in OPA_AUTH_CUSTOM_HEADERS, it will be overridden by OPA_AUTH_TOKEN.
OPA_AUTH_TOKEN.title: OPA authentication token

OPA_AUTH_TYPE.description:
The authentication type that will be used to connect to the OPA server: "None", "Token", or "Certificate".

* `None` - None
* `Token` - Token
* `Certificate` - Certificate
OPA_AUTH_TYPE.title: OPA authentication type

OPA_HOST.description: The hostname used to connect to the OPA server. If empty, policy enforcement will be disabled.
OPA_HOST.title: OPA server hostname

OPA_PORT.description: The port used to connect to the OPA server. Defaults to 8181.
OPA_PORT.title: OPA server port

OPA_REQUEST_RETRIES.description: The number of retry attempts for connecting to the OPA server. Default is 2.
OPA_REQUEST_RETRIES.title: OPA request retry count

OPA_REQUEST_TIMEOUT.description: The number of seconds after which the connection to the OPA server will time out. Defaults to 1.5 seconds.

OPA_SSL.description: Enable or disable the use of SSL to connect to the OPA server. Defaults to false.
OPA_SSL.title: Use SSL for OPA connection

ORG_ADMINS_CAN_SEE_ALL_USERS.description: Controls whether any Organization Admin can view all users and teams, even those not associated with their Organization.
ORG_ADMINS_CAN_SEE_ALL_USERS.title: All Users Visible to Organization Admins

PROJECT_UPDATE_VVV.description: Adds the CLI -vvv flag to ansible-playbook runs of project_update.yml used for project updates.
PROJECT_UPDATE_VVV.title: Run Project Updates With Higher Verbosity

PROXY_IP_ALLOWED_LIST.description: If the service is behind a reverse proxy/load balancer, use this setting to configure the proxy IP addresses from which the service should trust custom REMOTE_HOST_HEADERS header values. If this setting is an empty list (the default), the headers specified by REMOTE_HOST_HEADERS will be trusted unconditionally')

RADIUS_SECRET.description: Shared secret for authenticating to RADIUS server.

RADIUS_SERVER.description: Hostname/IP of RADIUS server. RADIUS authentication is disabled if this setting is empty.

RECEPTOR_KEEP_WORK_ON_ERROR.description: Prevent receptor work from being released on when error is detected
RECEPTOR_KEEP_WORK_ON_ERROR.title: Keep receptor work on error

REDHAT_PASSWORD.description: Client secret used to send data to Automation Analytics
REDHAT_PASSWORD.title: Red Hat Client Secret for Analytics

REDHAT_USERNAME.description: Client ID used to send data to Automation Analytics
REDHAT_USERNAME.title: Red Hat Client ID for Analytics

REMOTE_HOST_HEADERS.description: HTTP headers and meta keys to search to determine remote host name or IP. Add additional items to this list, such as "HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR", if behind a reverse proxy. See the "Proxy Support" section of the AAP Installation guide for more details.

SAML_AUTO_CREATE_OBJECTS.description: When enabled (the default), mapped Organizations and Teams will be created automatically on successful SAML login.
SAML_AUTO_CREATE_OBJECTS.title: Automatically Create Organizations and Teams on SAML Login

SCHEDULE_MAX_JOBS.description: Maximum number of the same job template that can be waiting to run when launching from a schedule before no more are created.
SCHEDULE_MAX_JOBS.title: Maximum Scheduled Jobs

SESSIONS_PER_USER.description: Maximum number of simultaneous logged in sessions a user may have. To disable enter -1.
SESSIONS_PER_USER.title: Maximum number of simultaneous logged in sessions

SESSION_COOKIE_AGE.description: Number of seconds that a user is inactive before they will need to login again.
SESSION_COOKIE_AGE.title: Idle Time Force Log Out

SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_OAUTH2_KEY.description: The OAuth2 key (Client ID) from your Azure AD application.
SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_OAUTH2_KEY.title: Azure AD OAuth2 Key

SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_OAUTH2_ORGANIZATION_.description:
Mapping to organization admins/users from social auth accounts. This setting
controls which users are placed into which organizations based on their
username and email address. Configuration details are available in the
documentation.
SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_OAUTH2_ORGANIZATION_.title: Azure AD OAuth2 Organization Map

SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_OAUTH2_SECRET.description: The OAuth2 secret (Client Secret) from your Azure AD application.
SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_OAUTH2_SECRET.title: Azure AD OAuth2 Secret

SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_OAUTH2_TEAM_MAP.description:
Mapping of team members (users) from social auth accounts. Configuration
details are available in the documentation.
SOCIAL_AUTH_AZUREAD_OAUTH2_TEAM_MAP.title: Azure AD OAuth2 Team Map

SOCIAL_AUTH_GITHUB_ENTERPRISE_API_URL.description: The API URL for your GitHub Enterprise instance, e.g.: http(s)://hostname/api/v3/. Refer to Github Enterprise documentation for more details.
SOCIAL_AUTH_GITHUB_ENTERPRISE_API_URL.title: GitHub Enterprise API URL

SOCIAL_AUTH_GITHUB_ENTERPRISE_KEY.description: The OAuth2 key (Client ID) from your GitHub Enterprise developer application.
SOCIAL_AUTH_GITHUB_ENTERPRISE_KEY.title: GitHub Enterprise OAuth2 Key

SOCIAL_AUTH_GITHUB_ENTERPRISE_ORGANIZATI.description:
Mapping to organization admins/users from social auth accounts. This setting
controls which users are placed into which organizations based on their
username and email address. Configuration details are available in the
documentation.
SOCIAL_AUTH_GITHUB_ENTERPRISE_ORGANIZATI.title: GitHub Enterprise OAuth2 Organization Map

SOCIAL_AUTH_GITHUB_ENTERPRISE_ORG_API_UR.description: The API URL for your GitHub Enterprise instance, e.g.: http(s)://hostname/api/v3/. Refer to Github Enterprise documentation for more details.

SettingSingleton.description: Present a group of settings (by category) as a single object.

ACTIVITY_STREAM_ENABLED.description: Enable capturing activity for the activity stream.
ACTIVITY_STREAM_ENABLED.title: Enable Activity Stream

ACTIVITY_STREAM_ENABLED_FOR_INVENTORY_SY.description: Enable capturing activity for the activity stream when running inventory sync.
ACTIVITY_STREAM_ENABLED_FOR_INVENTORY_SY.title: Enable Activity Stream for Inventory Sync

AD_HOC_COMMANDS.description: List of modules allowed to be used by ad-hoc jobs.
AD_HOC_COMMANDS.title: Ansible Modules Allowed for Ad Hoc Jobs

ALLOW_JINJA_IN_EXTRA_VARS.description:
Ansible allows variable substitution via the Jinja2 templating language for --extra-vars. This poses a potential security risk where users with the ability to specify extra vars at job launch time can use Jinja2 templates to run arbitrary Python.  It is recommended that this value be set to "template" or "never".

* `always` - Always
* `never` - Never
* `template` - Only On Job Template Definitions
ALLOW_JINJA_IN_EXTRA_VARS.title: When can extra variables contain Jinja templates?

ALLOW_METRICS_FOR_ANONYMOUS_USERS.description: If true, anonymous users are allowed to poll metrics.
ALLOW_METRICS_FOR_ANONYMOUS_USERS.title: Allow anonymous users to poll metrics

ALLOW_OAUTH2_FOR_EXTERNAL_USERS.description: For security reasons, users from external auth providers (LDAP, SAML, SSO, Radius, and others) are not allowed to create OAuth2 tokens. To change this behavior, enable this setting. Existing tokens will not be deleted when this setting is toggled off.
ALLOW_OAUTH2_FOR_EXTERNAL_USERS.title: Allow External Users to Create OAuth2 Tokens

ANSIBLE_FACT_CACHE_TIMEOUT.description: Maximum time, in seconds, that stored Ansible facts are considered valid since the last time they were modified. Only valid, non-stale, facts will be accessible by a playbook. Note, this does not influence the deletion of ansible_facts from the database. Use a value of 0 to indicate that no timeout should be imposed.
ANSIBLE_FACT_CACHE_TIMEOUT.title: Per-Host Ansible Fact Cache Timeout

API_400_ERROR_LOG_FORMAT.default: status {status_code} received by user {user_name} attempting to access {url_path} from {remote_addr}
API_400_ERROR_LOG_FORMAT.description:
The format of logged messages when an API 4XX error occurs, the following variables will be substituted: 
status_code - The HTTP status code of the error
user_name - The user name attempting to use the API
url_path - The URL path to the API endpoint called
remote_addr - The remote address seen for the user
error - The error set by the api endpoint
Variables need to be in the format {<variable name>}.
API_400_ERROR_LOG_FORMAT.title: Log Format For API 4XX Errors

AUTHENTICATION_BACKENDS.description: List of authentication backends that are enabled based on license features and other authentication settings.

AUTH_BASIC_ENABLED.description: Enable HTTP Basic Auth for the API Browser.
AUTH_BASIC_ENABLED.title: Enable HTTP Basic Auth

AUTH_LDAP_1_BIND_DN.description: DN (Distinguished Name) of user to bind for all search queries. This is the system user account we will use to login to query LDAP for other user information. Refer to the documentation for example syntax.
AUTH_LDAP_1_BIND_DN.title: LDAP Bind DN

AUTH_LDAP_1_BIND_PASSWORD.description: Password used to bind LDAP user account.
AUTH_LDAP_1_BIND_PASSWORD.title: LDAP Bind Password

AUTH_LDAP_1_CONNECTION_OPTIONS.description: Additional options to set for the LDAP connection.  LDAP referrals are disabled by default (to prevent certain LDAP queries from hanging with AD). Option names should be strings (e.g. "OPT_REFERRALS"). Refer to https://www.python-ldap.org/doc/html/ldap.html#options for possible options and values that can be set.
AUTH_LDAP_1_CONNECTION_OPTIONS.title: LDAP Connection Options

AUTH_LDAP_1_DENY_GROUP.description: Group DN denied from login. If specified, user will not be allowed to login if a member of this group.  Only one deny group is supported.
AUTH_LDAP_1_DENY_GROUP.title: LDAP Deny Group

AUTH_LDAP_1_GROUP_SEARCH.description: Users are mapped to organizations based on their membership in LDAP groups. This setting defines the LDAP search query to find groups. Unlike the user search, group search does not support LDAPSearchUnion.
AUTH_LDAP_1_GROUP_SEARCH.title: LDAP Group Search

AUTH_LDAP_1_GROUP_TYPE.description:
The group type may need to be changed based on the type of the LDAP server.  Values are listed at: https://django-auth-ldap.readthedocs.io/en/stable/groups.html#types-of-groups

* `PosixGroupType` - PosixGroupType
* `GroupOfNamesType` - GroupOfNamesType
* `GroupOfUniqueNamesType` - GroupOfUniqueNamesType
* `ActiveDirectoryGroupType` - ActiveDirectoryGroupType
* `OrganizationalRoleGroupType` - OrganizationalRoleGroupType
* `MemberDNGroupType` - MemberDNGroupType
* `NestedGroupOfNamesType` - NestedGroupOfNamesType
* `NestedGroupOfUniqueNamesType` - NestedGroupOfUniqueNamesType
* `NestedActiveDirectoryGroupType` - NestedActiveDirectoryGroupType
* `NestedOrganizationalRoleGroupType` - NestedOrganizationalRoleGroupType
* `NestedMemberDNGroupType` - NestedMemberDNGroupType
* `PosixUIDGroupType` - PosixUIDGroupType
AUTH_LDAP_1_GROUP_TYPE.title: LDAP Group Type

AUTH_LDAP_1_GROUP_TYPE_PARAMS.description: Key value parameters to send the chosen group type init method.
AUTH_LDAP_1_GROUP_TYPE_PARAMS.title: LDAP Group Type Parameters

AUTH_LDAP_1_ORGANIZATION_MAP.description: Mapping between organization admins/users and LDAP groups. This controls which users are placed into which organizations relative to their LDAP group memberships. Configuration details are available in the documentation.
AUTH_LDAP_1_ORGANIZATION_MAP.title: LDAP Organization Map

AUTH_LDAP_1_REQUIRE_GROUP.description: Group DN required to login. If specified, user must be a member of this group to login via LDAP. If not set, everyone in LDAP that matches the user search will be able to login to the service. Only one require group is supported.
AUTH_LDAP_1_REQUIRE_GROUP.title: LDAP Require Group

AUTH_LDAP_1_SERVER_URI.description: URI to connect to LDAP server, such as "ldap://ldap.example.com:389" (non-SSL) or "ldaps://ldap.example.com:636" (SSL). Multiple LDAP servers may be specified by separating with spaces or commas. LDAP authentication is disabled if this parameter is empty.
AUTH_LDAP_1_SERVER_URI.title: LDAP Server URI

AUTH_LDAP_1_START_TLS.description: Whether to enable TLS when the LDAP connection is not using SSL.
AUTH_LDAP_1_START_TLS.title: LDAP Start TLS

AUTH_LDAP_1_TEAM_MAP.description: Mapping between team members (users) and LDAP groups. Configuration details are available in the documentation.
AUTH_LDAP_1_TEAM_MAP.title: LDAP Team Map

AUTH_LDAP_1_USER_ATTR_MAP.description: Mapping of LDAP user schema to API user attributes. The default setting is valid for ActiveDirectory but users with other LDAP configurations may need to change the values. Refer to the documentation for additional details.
AUTH_LDAP_1_USER_ATTR_MAP.title: LDAP User Attribute Map

AUTH_LDAP_1_USER_DN_TEMPLATE.description: Alternative to user search, if user DNs are all of the same format. This approach is more efficient for user lookups than searching if it is usable in your organizational environment. If this setting has a value it will be used instead of AUTH_LDAP_USER_SEARCH.
AUTH_LDAP_1_USER_DN_TEMPLATE.title: LDAP User DN Template

AUTH_LDAP_1_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP.description: Retrieve users from a given group. At this time, superuser and system auditors are the only groups supported. Refer to the documentation for more detail.
AUTH_LDAP_1_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP.title: LDAP User Flags By Group

AUTH_LDAP_1_USER_SEARCH.description: LDAP search query to find users.  Any user that matches the given pattern will be able to login to the service.  The user should also be mapped into an organization (as defined in the AUTH_LDAP_ORGANIZATION_MAP setting).  If multiple search queries need to be supported use of "LDAPUnion" is possible. See the documentation for details.
AUTH_LDAP_1_USER_SEARCH.title: LDAP User Search

AUTH_LDAP_4_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP.description: Retrieve users from a given group. At this time, superuser and system auditors are the only groups supported. Refer to the documentation for more detail.
AUTH_LDAP_4_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP.title: LDAP User Flags By Group

AUTH_LDAP_4_USER_SEARCH.description: LDAP search query to find users.  Any user that matches the given pattern will be able to login to the service.  The user should also be mapped into an organization (as defined in the AUTH_LDAP_ORGANIZATION_MAP setting).  If multiple search queries need to be supported use of "LDAPUnion" is possible. See the documentation for details.
AUTH_LDAP_4_USER_SEARCH.title: LDAP User Search

AUTH_LDAP_5_BIND_DN.description: DN (Distinguished Name) of user to bind for all search queries. This is the system user account we will use to login to query LDAP for other user information. Refer to the documentation for example syntax.
AUTH_LDAP_5_BIND_DN.title: LDAP Bind DN

AUTH_LDAP_5_BIND_PASSWORD.description: Password used to bind LDAP user account.
AUTH_LDAP_5_BIND_PASSWORD.title: LDAP Bind Password

AUTH_LDAP_5_CONNECTION_OPTIONS.description: Additional options to set for the LDAP connection.  LDAP referrals are disabled by default (to prevent certain LDAP queries from hanging with AD). Option names should be strings (e.g. "OPT_REFERRALS"). Refer to https://www.python-ldap.org/doc/html/ldap.html#options for possible options and values that can be set.
AUTH_LDAP_5_CONNECTION_OPTIONS.title: LDAP Connection Options

AUTH_LDAP_5_DENY_GROUP.description: Group DN denied from login. If specified, user will not be allowed to login if a member of this group.  Only one deny group is supported.
AUTH_LDAP_5_DENY_GROUP.title: LDAP Deny Group

AUTH_LDAP_5_GROUP_SEARCH.description: Users are mapped to organizations based on their membership in LDAP groups. This setting defines the LDAP search query to find groups. Unlike the user search, group search does not support LDAPSearchUnion.
AUTH_LDAP_5_GROUP_SEARCH.title: LDAP Group Search

AUTH_LDAP_5_GROUP_TYPE.description:
The group type may need to be changed based on the type of the LDAP server.  Values are listed at: https://django-auth-ldap.readthedocs.io/en/stable/groups.html#types-of-groups

* `PosixGroupType` - PosixGroupType
* `GroupOfNamesType` - GroupOfNamesType
* `GroupOfUniqueNamesType` - GroupOfUniqueNamesType
* `ActiveDirectoryGroupType` - ActiveDirectoryGroupType
* `OrganizationalRoleGroupType` - OrganizationalRoleGroupType
* `MemberDNGroupType` - MemberDNGroupType
* `NestedGroupOfNamesType` - NestedGroupOfNamesType
* `NestedGroupOfUniqueNamesType` - NestedGroupOfUniqueNamesType
* `NestedActiveDirectoryGroupType` - NestedActiveDirectoryGroupType
* `NestedOrganizationalRoleGroupType` - NestedOrganizationalRoleGroupType
* `NestedMemberDNGroupType` - NestedMemberDNGroupType
* `PosixUIDGroupType` - PosixUIDGroupType
AUTH_LDAP_5_GROUP_TYPE.title: LDAP Group Type

AUTH_LDAP_5_GROUP_TYPE_PARAMS.description: Key value parameters to send the chosen group type init method.
AUTH_LDAP_5_GROUP_TYPE_PARAMS.title: LDAP Group Type Parameters

AUTH_LDAP_5_ORGANIZATION_MAP.description: Mapping between organization admins/users and LDAP groups. This controls which users are placed into which organizations relative to their LDAP group memberships. Configuration details are available in the documentation.
AUTH_LDAP_5_ORGANIZATION_MAP.title: LDAP Organization Map

AUTH_LDAP_5_REQUIRE_GROUP.description: Group DN required to login. If specified, user must be a member of this group to login via LDAP. If not set, everyone in LDAP that matches the user search will be able to login to the service. Only one require group is supported.
AUTH_LDAP_5_REQUIRE_GROUP.title: LDAP Require Group

AUTH_LDAP_5_SERVER_URI.description: URI to connect to LDAP server, such as "ldap://ldap.example.com:389" (non-SSL) or "ldaps://ldap.example.com:636" (SSL). Multiple LDAP servers may be specified by separating with spaces or commas. LDAP authentication is disabled if this parameter is empty.
AUTH_LDAP_5_SERVER_URI.title: LDAP Server URI

AUTH_LDAP_5_START_TLS.description: Whether to enable TLS when the LDAP connection is not using SSL.
AUTH_LDAP_5_START_TLS.title: LDAP Start TLS

AUTH_LDAP_5_TEAM_MAP.description: Mapping between team members (users) and LDAP groups. Configuration details are available in the documentation.
AUTH_LDAP_5_TEAM_MAP.title: LDAP Team Map

AUTH_LDAP_5_USER_ATTR_MAP.description: Mapping of LDAP user schema to API user attributes. The default setting is valid for ActiveDirectory but users with other LDAP configurations may need to change the values. Refer to the documentation for additional details.
AUTH_LDAP_5_USER_ATTR_MAP.title: LDAP User Attribute Map

AUTH_LDAP_5_USER_DN_TEMPLATE.description: Alternative to user search, if user DNs are all of the same format. This approach is more efficient for user lookups than searching if it is usable in your organizational environment. If this setting has a value it will be used instead of AUTH_LDAP_USER_SEARCH.
AUTH_LDAP_5_USER_DN_TEMPLATE.title: LDAP User DN Template

AUTH_LDAP_5_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP.description: Retrieve users from a given group. At this time, superuser and system auditors are the only groups supported. Refer to the documentation for more detail.
AUTH_LDAP_5_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP.title: LDAP User Flags By Group

AUTH_LDAP_5_USER_SEARCH.description: LDAP search query to find users.  Any user that matches the given pattern will be able to login to the service.  The user should also be mapped into an organization (as defined in the AUTH_LDAP_ORGANIZATION_MAP setting).  If multiple search queries need to be supported use of "LDAPUnion" is possible. See the documentation for details.
AUTH_LDAP_5_USER_SEARCH.title: LDAP User Search

AUTH_LDAP_BIND_DN.description: DN (Distinguished Name) of user to bind for all search queries. This is the system user account we will use to login to query LDAP for other user information. Refer to the documentation for example syntax.
AUTH_LDAP_BIND_DN.title: LDAP Bind DN

AUTH_LDAP_BIND_PASSWORD.description: Password used to bind LDAP user account.
AUTH_LDAP_BIND_PASSWORD.title: LDAP Bind Password

AUTH_LDAP_CONNECTION_OPTIONS.description: Additional options to set for the LDAP connection.  LDAP referrals are disabled by default (to prevent certain LDAP queries from hanging with AD). Option names should be strings (e.g. "OPT_REFERRALS"). Refer to https://www.python-ldap.org/doc/html/ldap.html#options for possible options and values that can be set.
AUTH_LDAP_CONNECTION_OPTIONS.title: LDAP Connection Options

AUTH_LDAP_DENY_GROUP.description: Group DN denied from login. If specified, user will not be allowed to login if a member of this group.  Only one deny group is supported.
AUTH_LDAP_DENY_GROUP.title: LDAP Deny Group

AUTH_LDAP_GROUP_SEARCH.description: Users are mapped to organizations based on their membership in LDAP groups. This setting defines the LDAP search query to find groups. Unlike the user search, group search does not support LDAPSearchUnion.
AUTH_LDAP_GROUP_SEARCH.title: LDAP Group Search

AUTH_LDAP_GROUP_TYPE.description:
The group type may need to be changed based on the type of the LDAP server.  Values are listed at: https://django-auth-ldap.readthedocs.io/en/stable/groups.html#types-of-groups

* `PosixGroupType` - PosixGroupType
* `GroupOfNamesType` - GroupOfNamesType
* `GroupOfUniqueNamesType` - GroupOfUniqueNamesType
* `ActiveDirectoryGroupType` - ActiveDirectoryGroupType
* `OrganizationalRoleGroupType` - OrganizationalRoleGroupType
* `MemberDNGroupType` - MemberDNGroupType
* `NestedGroupOfNamesType` - NestedGroupOfNamesType
* `NestedGroupOfUniqueNamesType` - NestedGroupOfUniqueNamesType
* `NestedActiveDirectoryGroupType` - NestedActiveDirectoryGroupType
* `NestedOrganizationalRoleGroupType` - NestedOrganizationalRoleGroupType
* `NestedMemberDNGroupType` - NestedMemberDNGroupType
* `PosixUIDGroupType` - PosixUIDGroupType
AUTH_LDAP_GROUP_TYPE.title: LDAP Group Type

AUTH_LDAP_GROUP_TYPE_PARAMS.description: Key value parameters to send the chosen group type init method.
AUTH_LDAP_GROUP_TYPE_PARAMS.title: LDAP Group Type Parameters

AUTH_LDAP_ORGANIZATION_MAP.description: Mapping between organization admins/users and LDAP groups. This controls which users are placed into which organizations relative to their LDAP group memberships. Configuration details are available in the documentation.
AUTH_LDAP_ORGANIZATION_MAP.title: LDAP Organization Map

AUTH_LDAP_REQUIRE_GROUP.description: Group DN required to login. If specified, user must be a member of this group to login via LDAP. If not set, everyone in LDAP that matches the user search will be able to login to the service. Only one require group is supported.
AUTH_LDAP_REQUIRE_GROUP.title: LDAP Require Group

AUTH_LDAP_SERVER_URI.description: URI to connect to LDAP server, such as "ldap://ldap.example.com:389" (non-SSL) or "ldaps://ldap.example.com:636" (SSL). Multiple LDAP servers may be specified by separating with spaces or commas. LDAP authentication is disabled if this parameter is empty.
AUTH_LDAP_SERVER_URI.title: LDAP Server URI

AUTH_LDAP_START_TLS.description: Whether to enable TLS when the LDAP connection is not using SSL.
AUTH_LDAP_START_TLS.title: LDAP Start TLS

AUTH_LDAP_TEAM_MAP.description: Mapping between team members (users) and LDAP groups. Configuration details are available in the documentation.
AUTH_LDAP_TEAM_MAP.title: LDAP Team Map

AUTH_LDAP_USER_ATTR_MAP.description: Mapping of LDAP user schema to API user attributes. The default setting is valid for ActiveDirectory but users with other LDAP configurations may need to change the values. Refer to the documentation for additional details.
AUTH_LDAP_USER_ATTR_MAP.title: LDAP User Attribute Map

AUTH_LDAP_USER_DN_TEMPLATE.description: Alternative to user search, if user DNs are all of the same format. This approach is more efficient for user lookups than searching if it is usable in your organizational environment. If this setting has a value it will be used instead of AUTH_LDAP_USER_SEARCH.
AUTH_LDAP_USER_DN_TEMPLATE.title: LDAP User DN Template

AUTH_LDAP_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP.description: Retrieve users from a given group. At this time, superuser and system auditors are the only groups supported. Refer to the documentation for more detail.
AUTH_LDAP_USER_FLAGS_BY_GROUP.title: LDAP User Flags By Group

AUTH_LDAP_USER_SEARCH.description: LDAP search query to find users.  Any user that matches the given pattern will be able to login to the service.  The user should also be mapped into an organization (as defined in the AUTH_LDAP_ORGANIZATION_MAP setting).  If multiple search queries need to be supported use of "LDAPUnion" is possible. See the documentation for details.
AUTH_LDAP_USER_SEARCH.title: LDAP User Search

AUTOMATION_ANALYTICS_LAST_ENTRIES.title: Last gathered entries from the data collection service of Automation Analytics

AUTOMATION_ANALYTICS_LAST_GATHER.title: Last gather date for Automation Analytics.

AUTOMATION_ANALYTICS_URL.description: This setting is used to to configure the upload URL for data collection for Automation Analytics.
AUTOMATION_ANALYTICS_URL.title: Automation Analytics upload URL

AWX_ANSIBLE_CALLBACK_PLUGINS.description: List of paths to search for extra callback plugins to be used when running jobs. Enter one path per line.
AWX_ANSIBLE_CALLBACK_PLUGINS.title: Ansible Callback Plugins

AWX_CLEANUP_PATHS.description: Enable or Disable TMP Dir cleanup
AWX_CLEANUP_PATHS.title: Enable or Disable tmp dir cleanup

AWX_COLLECTIONS_ENABLED.description: Allows collections to be dynamically downloaded from a requirements.yml file for SCM projects.
AWX_COLLECTIONS_ENABLED.title: Enable Collection(s) Download

AWX_ISOLATION_BASE_PATH.description: The directory in which the service will create new temporary directories for job execution and isolation (such as credential files).
AWX_ISOLATION_BASE_PATH.title: Job execution path

AWX_ISOLATION_SHOW_PATHS.description: List of paths that would otherwise be hidden to expose to isolated jobs. Enter one path per line. Volumes will be mounted from the execution node to the container. The supported format is HOST-DIR[:CONTAINER-DIR[:OPTIONS]].
AWX_ISOLATION_SHOW_PATHS.title: Paths to expose to isolated jobs

AWX_MOUNT_ISOLATED_PATHS_ON_K8S.description: Expose paths via hostPath for the Pods created by a Container Group. HostPath volumes present many security risks, and it is a best practice to avoid the use of HostPaths when possible.
AWX_MOUNT_ISOLATED_PATHS_ON_K8S.title: Expose host paths for Container Groups

AWX_ROLES_ENABLED.description: Allows roles to be dynamically downloaded from a requirements.yml file for SCM projects.
AWX_ROLES_ENABLED.title: Enable Role Download

AWX_RUNNER_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS.description: Only applies to jobs running in a Container Group. If not 0, send a message every so-many seconds to keep connection open.
AWX_RUNNER_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS.title: K8S Ansible Runner Keep-Alive Message Interval

AWX_SHOW_PLAYBOOK_LINKS.description: Follow symbolic links when scanning for playbooks. Be aware that setting this to True can lead to infinite recursion if a link points to a parent directory of itself.
AWX_SHOW_PLAYBOOK_LINKS.title: Follow symlinks

AWX_TASK_ENV.description: Additional environment variables set for playbook runs, inventory updates, project updates, and notification sending.
AWX_TASK_ENV.title: Extra Environment Variables

BULK_HOST_MAX_CREATE.description: Max number of hosts to allow to be created in a single bulk action
BULK_HOST_MAX_CREATE.title: Max number of hosts to allow to be created in a single bulk action

BULK_HOST_MAX_DELETE.description: Max number of hosts to allow to be deleted in a single bulk action
BULK_HOST_MAX_DELETE.title: Max number of hosts to allow to be deleted in a single bulk action

BULK_JOB_MAX_LAUNCH.description: Max jobs to allow bulk jobs to launch
BULK_JOB_MAX_LAUNCH.title: Max jobs to allow bulk jobs to launch

CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS.description: If the service is behind a reverse proxy/load balancer, use this setting to configure the schema://addresses from which the service should trust Origin header values.
CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS.title: CSRF Trusted Origins List

CUSTOM_LOGIN_INFO.description: If needed, you can add specific information (such as a legal notice or a disclaimer) to a text box in the login modal using this setting. Any content added must be in plain text or an HTML fragment, as other markup languages are not supported.

CUSTOM_LOGO.description: To set up a custom logo, provide a file that you create. For the custom logo to look its best, use a .png file with a transparent background. GIF, PNG and JPEG formats are supported.

CUSTOM_VENV_PATHS.description: Paths where Tower will look for custom virtual environments (in addition to /var/lib/awx/venv/). Enter one path per line.
CUSTOM_VENV_PATHS.title: Custom virtual environment paths

DEFAULT_CONTAINER_RUN_OPTIONS.description: List of options to pass to podman run example: ['--network', 'slirp4netns:enable_ipv6=true', '--log-level', 'debug']
DEFAULT_CONTAINER_RUN_OPTIONS.title: Container Run Options

DEFAULT_CONTROL_PLANE_QUEUE_NAME.title: The instance group where control plane tasks run

DEFAULT_EXECUTION_ENVIRONMENT.description: The Execution Environment to be used when one has not been configured for a job template.
DEFAULT_EXECUTION_ENVIRONMENT.title: Global default execution environment

DEFAULT_EXECUTION_QUEUE_NAME.title: The instance group where user jobs run (currently only on non-VM installs)

DEFAULT_INVENTORY_UPDATE_TIMEOUT.description: Maximum time in seconds to allow inventory updates to run. Use value of 0 to indicate that no timeout should be imposed. A timeout set on an individual inventory source will override this.

DEFAULT_JOB_IDLE_TIMEOUT.description: If no output is detected from ansible in this number of seconds the execution will be terminated. Use value of 0 to indicate that no idle timeout should be imposed.

DEFAULT_JOB_TIMEOUT.description: Maximum time in seconds to allow jobs to run. Use value of 0 to indicate that no timeout should be imposed. A timeout set on an individual job template will override this.

DEFAULT_PROJECT_UPDATE_TIMEOUT.description: Maximum time in seconds to allow project updates to run. Use value of 0 to indicate that no timeout should be imposed. A timeout set on an individual project will override this.

DISABLE_LOCAL_AUTH.description: Controls whether users are prevented from using the built-in authentication system. You probably want to do this if you are using an LDAP or SAML integration.
DISABLE_LOCAL_AUTH.title: Disable the built-in authentication system

EVENT_STDOUT_MAX_BYTES_DISPLAY.description: Maximum Size of Standard Output in bytes to display for a single job or ad hoc command event. `stdout` will end with `…` when truncated.
EVENT_STDOUT_MAX_BYTES_DISPLAY.title: Job Event Standard Output Maximum Display Size

GALAXY_IGNORE_CERTS.description: If set to true, certificate validation will not be done when installing content from any Galaxy server.
GALAXY_IGNORE_CERTS.title: Ignore Ansible Galaxy SSL Certificate Verification

GALAXY_TASK_ENV.description: Additional environment variables set for invocations of ansible-galaxy within project updates. Useful if you must use a proxy server for ansible-galaxy but not git.
GALAXY_TASK_ENV.title: Environment Variables for Galaxy Commands

INSIGHTS_TRACKING_STATE.description: Enables the service to gather data on automation and send it to Automation Analytics.
INSIGHTS_TRACKING_STATE.title: Gather data for Automation Analytics

IS_K8S.description: Indicates whether the instance is part of a kubernetes-based deployment.

LICENSE.description: The license controls which features and functionality are enabled. Use /api/v2/config/ to update or change the license.

LOCAL_PASSWORD_MIN_DIGITS.description: Minimum number of digit characters required in a local password. 0 means no minimum
LOCAL_PASSWORD_MIN_DIGITS.title: Minimum number of digit characters in local password

LOCAL_PASSWORD_MIN_LENGTH.description: Minimum number of characters required in a local password. 0 means no minimum
LOCAL_PASSWORD_MIN_LENGTH.title: Minimum number of characters in local password

LOCAL_PASSWORD_MIN_SPECIAL.description: Minimum number of special characters required in a local password. 0 means no minimum
LOCAL_PASSWORD_MIN_SPECIAL.title: Minimum number of special characters in local password

LOCAL_PASSWORD_MIN_UPPER.description: Minimum number of uppercase characters required in a local password. 0 means no minimum
LOCAL_PASSWORD_MIN_UPPER.title: Minimum number of uppercase characters in local password

LOGIN_REDIRECT_OVERRIDE.description: URL to which unauthorized users will be redirected to log in.  If blank, users will be sent to the login page.
LOGIN_REDIRECT_OVERRIDE.title: Login redirect override URL

LOG_AGGREGATOR_ACTION_MAX_DISK_USAGE_GB.description: Amount of data to store (in gigabytes) if an rsyslog action takes time to process an incoming message (defaults to 1). Equivalent to the rsyslogd queue.maxdiskspace setting on the action (e.g. omhttp). It stores files in the directory specified by LOG_AGGREGATOR_MAX_DISK_USAGE_PATH.
LOG_AGGREGATOR_ACTION_MAX_DISK_USAGE_GB.title: Maximum disk persistence for rsyslogd action queuing (in GB)

LOG_AGGREGATOR_ACTION_QUEUE_SIZE.description: Defines how large the rsyslog action queue can grow in number of messages stored. This can have an impact on memory utilization. When the queue reaches 75% of this number, the queue will start writing to disk (queue.highWatermark in rsyslog). When it reaches 90%, NOTICE, INFO, and DEBUG messages will start to be discarded (queue.discardMark with queue.discardSeverity=5).
LOG_AGGREGATOR_ACTION_QUEUE_SIZE.title: Maximum number of messages that can be stored in the log action queue

LOG_AGGREGATOR_ENABLED.description: Enable sending logs to external log aggregator.
LOG_AGGREGATOR_ENABLED.title: Enable External Logging

LOG_AGGREGATOR_HOST.description: Hostname/IP where external logs will be sent to.
LOG_AGGREGATOR_HOST.title: Logging Aggregator

LOG_AGGREGATOR_INDIVIDUAL_FACTS.description: If set, system tracking facts will be sent for each package, service, or other item found in a scan, allowing for greater search query granularity. If unset, facts will be sent as a single dictionary, allowing for greater efficiency in fact processing.
LOG_AGGREGATOR_INDIVIDUAL_FACTS.title: Log System Tracking Facts Individually

LOG_AGGREGATOR_LEVEL.description:
Level threshold used by log handler. Severities from lowest to highest are DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL. Messages less severe than the threshold will be ignored by log handler. (messages under category awx.anlytics ignore this setting)

* `DEBUG` - DEBUG
* `INFO` - INFO
* `WARNING` - WARNING
* `ERROR` - ERROR
* `CRITICAL` - CRITICAL
LOG_AGGREGATOR_LEVEL.title: Logging Aggregator Level Threshold

LOG_AGGREGATOR_LOGGERS.description:
List of loggers that will send HTTP logs to the collector, these can include any or all of: 
awx - service logs
activity_stream - activity stream records
job_events - callback data from Ansible job events
system_tracking - facts gathered from scan jobs
broadcast_websocket - errors pertaining to websockets broadcast metrics
job_lifecycle - logs related to processing of a job
LOG_AGGREGATOR_LOGGERS.title: Loggers Sending Data to Log Aggregator Form

LOG_AGGREGATOR_MAX_DISK_USAGE_PATH.description: Location to persist logs that should be retried after an outage of the external log aggregator (defaults to /var/lib/awx). Equivalent to the rsyslogd queue.spoolDirectory setting.
LOG_AGGREGATOR_MAX_DISK_USAGE_PATH.title: File system location for rsyslogd disk persistence

LOG_AGGREGATOR_PASSWORD.description: Password or authentication token for external log aggregator (if required; HTTP/s only).
LOG_AGGREGATOR_PASSWORD.title: Logging Aggregator Password/Token

LOG_AGGREGATOR_PORT.description: Port on Logging Aggregator to send logs to (if required and not provided in Logging Aggregator).
LOG_AGGREGATOR_PORT.title: Logging Aggregator Port

LOG_AGGREGATOR_PROTOCOL.description:
Protocol used to communicate with log aggregator.  HTTPS/HTTP assumes HTTPS unless http:// is explicitly used in the Logging Aggregator hostname.

* `https` - HTTPS/HTTP
* `tcp` - TCP
* `udp` - UDP
LOG_AGGREGATOR_PROTOCOL.title: Logging Aggregator Protocol

LOG_AGGREGATOR_RSYSLOGD_DEBUG.description: Enabled high verbosity debugging for rsyslogd.  Useful for debugging connection issues for external log aggregation.
LOG_AGGREGATOR_RSYSLOGD_DEBUG.title: Enable rsyslogd debugging

LOG_AGGREGATOR_TCP_TIMEOUT.description: Number of seconds for a TCP connection to external log aggregator to timeout. Applies to HTTPS and TCP log aggregator protocols.
LOG_AGGREGATOR_TCP_TIMEOUT.title: TCP Connection Timeout

LOG_AGGREGATOR_TYPE.description:
Format messages for the chosen log aggregator.

* `logstash` - logstash
* `splunk` - splunk
* `loggly` - loggly
* `sumologic` - sumologic
* `other` - other
LOG_AGGREGATOR_TYPE.title: Logging Aggregator Type

LOG_AGGREGATOR_USERNAME.description: Username for external log aggregator (if required; HTTP/s only).
LOG_AGGREGATOR_USERNAME.title: Logging Aggregator Username

LOG_AGGREGATOR_VERIFY_CERT.description: Flag to control enable/disable of certificate verification when LOG_AGGREGATOR_PROTOCOL is "https". If enabled, the log handler will verify certificate sent by external log aggregator before establishing connection.
LOG_AGGREGATOR_VERIFY_CERT.title: Enable/disable HTTPS certificate verification

MANAGE_ORGANIZATION_AUTH.description: Controls whether any Organization Admin has the privileges to create and manage users and teams. You may want to disable this ability if you are using an LDAP or SAML integration.
MANAGE_ORGANIZATION_AUTH.title: Organization Admins Can Manage Users and Teams

MAX_FORKS.description: Saving a Job Template with more than this number of forks will result in an error. When set to 0, no limit is applied.
MAX_FORKS.title: Maximum number of forks per job

MAX_UI_JOB_EVENTS.description: Maximum number of job events for the UI to retrieve within a single request.
MAX_UI_JOB_EVENTS.title: Max Job Events Retrieved by UI

MAX_WEBSOCKET_EVENT_RATE.description: Maximum number of messages to update the UI live job output with per second. Value of 0 means no limit.
MAX_WEBSOCKET_EVENT_RATE.title: Job Event Maximum Websocket Messages Per Second

NAMED_URL_FORMATS.description: Read-only list of key-value pairs that shows the standard format of all available named URLs.
NAMED_URL_FORMATS.title: Formats of all available named urls

NAMED_URL_GRAPH_NODES.description: Read-only list of key-value pairs that exposes named URL graph topology. Use this list to programmatically generate named URLs for resources
NAMED_URL_GRAPH_NODES.title: List of all named url graph nodes.

OAUTH2_PROVIDER.description: DEPRECATED: This setting is for a legacy feature and will be removed. Use token-based authentication instead.Dictionary for customizing OAuth 2 timeouts, available items are `ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_SECONDS`, the duration of access tokens in the number of seconds, `AUTHORIZATION_CODE_EXPIRE_SECONDS`, the duration of authorization codes in the number of seconds, and `REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRE_SECONDS`, the duration of refresh tokens, after expired access tokens, in the number of seconds.
OAUTH2_PROVIDER.title: OAuth 2 Timeout Settings

OPA_AUTH_CA_CERT.description: The content of the CA certificate for mTLS authentication to the OPA server. Required when OPA_AUTH_TYPE is "Certificate".
OPA_AUTH_CA_CERT.title: OPA CA certificate content

OPA_AUTH_CLIENT_CERT.description: The content of the client certificate file for mTLS authentication to the OPA server. Required when OPA_AUTH_TYPE is "Certificate".
OPA_AUTH_CLIENT_CERT.title: OPA client certificate content

OPA_AUTH_CLIENT_KEY.description: The content of the client key for mTLS authentication to the OPA server. Required when OPA_AUTH_TYPE is "Certificate".
OPA_AUTH_CLIENT_KEY.title: OPA client key content

OPA_AUTH_CUSTOM_HEADERS.description: Optional custom headers included in requests to the OPA server. Defaults to empty dictionary ({}).
OPA_AUTH_CUSTOM_HEADERS.title: OPA custom authentication headers

OPA_AUTH_TOKEN.description: The token for authentication to the OPA server. Required when OPA_AUTH_TYPE is "Token". If an authorization header is defined in OPA_AUTH_CUSTOM_HEADERS, it will be overridden by OPA_AUTH_TOKEN.
OPA_AUTH_TOKEN.title: OPA authentication token

OPA_AUTH_TYPE.description:
The authentication type that will be used to connect to the OPA server: "None", "Token", or "Certificate".

* `None` - None
* `Token` - Token
* `Certificate` - Certificate
OPA_AUTH_TYPE.title: OPA authentication type

OPA_HOST.description: The hostname used to connect to the OPA server. If empty, policy enforcement will be disabled.
OPA_HOST.title: OPA server hostname

OPA_PORT.description: The port used to connect to the OPA server. Defaults to 8181.
OPA_PORT.title: OPA server port

OPA_REQUEST_RETRIES.description: The number of retry attempts for connecting to the OPA server. Default is 2.
OPA_REQUEST_RETRIES.title: OPA request retry count

OPA_REQUEST_TIMEOUT.description: The number of seconds after which the connection to the OPA server will time out. Defaults to 1.5 seconds.

OPA_SSL.description: Enable or disable the use of SSL to connect to the OPA server. Defaults to false.
OPA_SSL.title: Use SSL for OPA connection

ORG_ADMINS_CAN_SEE_ALL_USERS.description: Controls whether any Organization Admin can view all users and teams, even those not associated with their Organization.
ORG_ADMINS_CAN_SEE_ALL_USERS.title: All Users Visible to Organization Admins

PENDO_TRACKING_STATE.description:
Enable or Disable User Analytics Tracking.

* `off` - Off
* `anonymous` - Anonymous
* `detailed` - Detailed
PENDO_TRACKING_STATE.title: User Analytics Tracking State

PROJECT_UPDATE_VVV.description: Adds the CLI -vvv flag to ansible-playbook runs of project_update.yml used for project updates.
PROJECT_UPDATE_VVV.title: Run Project Updates With Higher Verbosity

PROXY_IP_ALLOWED_LIST.description: If the service is behind a reverse proxy/load balancer, use this setting to configure the proxy IP addresses from which the service should trust custom REMOTE_HOST_HEADERS header values. If this setting is an empty list (the default), the headers specified by REMOTE_HOST_HEADERS will be trusted unconditionally')

REMOTE_HOST_HEADERS.description: HTTP headers and meta keys to search to determine remote host name or IP. Add additional items to this list, such as "HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR", if behind a reverse proxy. See the "Proxy Support" section of the AAP Installation guide for more details.

Choose the authentication protocol used by TACACS+ client.

* `ascii` - ascii
* `pap` - pap
TACACSPLUS_AUTH_PROTOCOL.title: TACACS+ Authentication Protocol
